articles.title: Supermajor Warns Oil Prices Could Hit $160 Within Weeks
articles.description: Exxon and Chevron executives warn at Bernstein conference that near-record-low oil inventories will push Brent prices to $150-$160 within weeks.

articles.title: Stocks rally continues on US-Iran peace deal hopes, AI spending optimism
articles.description: Crude oil prices dropped again, with West Texas Intermediate falling to around $87 a barrel as the US and Iran reached a preliminary deal to extend the ceasefire by another 60 days, though President Donald Trump has yet to agree to terms.

articles.title: Gas prices are easing: Where to buy it for under $4 a gallon in central Pa.
articles.description: The national average for a gallon of regular gasoline is down 12 cents from last week to $4.42 on Friday, May 29.

articles.title: Oil prices plummet as Trump announces “breakthrough” in Iran deal talks
articles.description: President Donald Trump surprised the world by saying that a deal with Iran—including reopening the critical Strait of Hormuz—was “largely negotiated.” This news caused oil prices to fall dramatically because traders believed global fuel supplies might soon flow freely again. The proposed agreement includes an official end to the war, followed by two months of […]

articles.title: UK petrol station plunges into liquidation - forecourt abandoned
articles.description: A UK based petrol station has fallen into liquidation in a dent for petrol and diesel car owners.

articles.title: After petrol, diesel, and milk, THIS essential product becomes costlier; prices increased by up to 5 percent
articles.description: This latest price increase comes at a time when FMCG companies are struggling with continuous inflationary pressure in commodities, packaging materials, and logistics costs.

articles.title: US Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq Edge Higher As Oil Cools; Traders Await Iran Truce Deal
articles.description: Crude oil prices eased a bit with Brent crude down 1.71% to $92.11 per barrel.

articles.title: World Bank sees Brent oil averaging US$86 in 2026, easing to US$70 in 2027
articles.description: KUALA LUMPUR: The World Bank Group has forecast that Brent oil prices will remain elevated in the near term before easing, averaging US$86 (US$1=RM3.96) per barrel in 2026 and US$70 per barrel in 2027.

articles.title: Wall Street inches higher, oil prices fall on hope of ceasefire extension
articles.description: Oil prices are down and markets are inching higher before the opening bell, adding to records hit a day earlier on optimism over a tentative deal to extend the ceasefire in the war with Iran.
